title: Agua de Beber
source: midisfree.com (full-length transcription, 86 measures, bossa guitar+flute; replaced the 1,855-byte 8-measure bitmidi uploads/4374 fragment — a mislabeled trance track — 2026-07-09 data-audit follow-up)

musicalLanguage.description: Antonio Carlos Jobim's Agua de Beber appears here as an 86-measure bossa nova transcription at 100 BPM, unusually transparent in texture: 1,129 notes in all, with no measure carrying more than 12 onsets — the quiet, conversational density this style favors. The right hand tops out at A5 in measure 21 over a left hand that never drops below E2. The harmony data is dominated by one implied color: 19 of the 32 confidence-gated windows suggest an A minor seventh sonority, with implied C7, C major seventh, and B-rooted windows scattered between — labels cover 36% of the windows the analysis considered, the stated D minor fits the whole-song profile only weakly (0.113), and detection is itself ambiguous (0.045 margin), so every harmonic claim here stays inferred. The large-scale design is generous: the right hand's melodic line of measures 24-43 returns with zero transposition at measures 44-63.
musicalLanguage.structure: The section lens divides the piece into 8 regions, with its strongest interior pivots at measure 26 (novelty 2.48) and measure 80 (novelty 2.19). Inside that frame the repetition is dense: a four-measure phrase at measures 32-35 returns byte-identical at measures 36-39, 40-43, 52-55, 56-59, and 60-63 — six statements in all — while the comping measures of the opening (measures 1-5) resurface at measures 65-80 in a mix of byte-identical and near-identical returns. The pattern lens confirms the big picture: the right hand's 87-note line spanning measures 24-43 restates at 44-63 (47% of right-hand onsets), and the left hand's 91-note line of measures 25-44 restates at 45-64. The final region (measures 80-86) thins steadily to a single left-hand onset at measure 86.
keyMoments: Measures 32-35 form a four-measure vamp that returns byte-identical at measures 36-39, 40-43, 52-55, 56-59, and 60-63 — six statements in all, so learning these four measures once unlocks 24 of the piece's 86 measures.

The busiest playing in the whole file is just 12 onsets per measure (measures 35, 39, 43, 55, and 59) — a lesson that bossa nova intensity comes from syncopation and placement, never from piling on notes.

19 of the 32 confidence-gated chord windows suggest an implied A minor seventh color, and the analysis hedges every one of them — with whole-song detection ambiguous (0.045 margin) and the stated D minor fitting weakly (0.113), the harmony here asks to be confirmed by ear rather than read as fact.

The piece empties out at the close: the right hand's longest rest is the final measure 86 itself, which carries a single left-hand onset — a one-measure dying-away ending after 85 measures of continuous motion.
teachingGoals: Practice the four-measure vamp at measures 32-35 until it cycles without thought — its six byte-identical statements across measures 32-43 and 52-63 cover 24 of the piece's 86 measures.

Learn the melodic line of measures 24-43 as one 20-measure arc, then recognize its exact return at measures 44-63 — the pattern lens shows the right hand's line repeating there with zero transposition, covering 47% of the right hand's onsets.

Hear the implied A minor seventh that dominates the labeled windows (19 of 32) as a resting sonority rather than a functional progression — every window is hedged as inferred, so let your ear confirm what the labels only suggest.

Build the left hand's short comping cell first: a seven-note figure recurs at ten locations (measures 2-3, 4-5, 10-11, 12-13, and again from measure 64 onward), compressing much of the accompaniment into one learnable shape.
styleTips: Keep the eighth notes straight, not swung — bossa nova's lilt comes from syncopation against an even pulse; listen for the left hand's steady low A2 anchors holding while the right hand's chords land between the beats.

Play the whole piece inside a small dynamic envelope — no measure of the 86 exceeds 12 onsets, so shape phrases with touch and timing rather than volume; listen for every chord attack staying soft enough that the melody's longer notes ring over it.
Let measure 86 genuinely die away: after 85 measures of motion the file ends on one left-hand note — feel the silence around that final onset as part of the piece, not an early release.

Use the detected section boundaries (new regions open at measures 26, 48, 63, and 80) as breathing points; listen for the texture change at measure 26, the strongest interior pivot the section lens reports (novelty 2.48).
